title: NBS T198 historical Manila-rope construction and test-design evidence
evidence_domain: non-operational historical commercial three-strand regular-lay Manila-rope construction and test-design evidence
historical_status: primary Bureau of Standards report issued in 1921 from commercial procurement-test data accumulated around its study period
rights_scope: eligible employee-authored historical text only; catalog supports identity; rights page supports rights provenance; every third-party or uncertain component excluded
modification_notice: Manually rewritten, narrowed and safety-bounded on 2026-07-16; republished courtesy of the National Institute of Standards and Technology.
retained_scope: T198 identity, authorship, historical status and bounded sample identity
historical yarn, strand, rope-lay, strand-lay, regular-lay and hard/soft-laid vocabulary
sampling, observation-coverage, specimen-preparation and measurement distinctions at non-operational resolution
termination-region failure, incomplete provenance, measurement limits and other negative evidence
absolute prohibition on modern material, operational, bondage or human-suspension transfer
excluded_scope: every raw breaking-load or strength value, fitted or calculated result, force, dimension, coefficient and unit-bearing performance value
every formula, equation, numerical table, plot, tolerance, ranking, acceptance threshold, procurement rule and period safe-use claim
all photographs, figures, captions, labels, visual marks and image-derived claims
all specimen-preparation dimensions, conditioning duration, fastening, machine, preload, measuring and handling recipes
standards, purchase specifications, commercial labels, manufacturer remarks and external source bodies
modern rope selection, care, hygiene, retirement, knots, working loads, body contact, uplines, anchors, bondage and human suspension
